title: Quality and Outliers
text:
In order to provide the most accurate version of this essential data, we performed two levels of checks. First, we set up and enforced basic boundary rules to ensure the data was within reasonable bounds. Second, we set up and ran statistical analysis in order to drop extreme anamolies within the data. For transparency, logs of the the removed records are shown below.

Quality
Quality checks were performed on raw data in order to enforce boundaries. The logs of flagged/removed values may be seen below, organized by month and table (rate_line, header).

Outliers
Outlier detection was performed on Level 2 data in order to identify and NULL extreme values. The log of flagged/removed values may be seen below, outputted from the multi-year consolidated dataset.








File Name
Size
Download




l3_pce_outliers_log.csv
402K
Download CSV

title: Data
text:
Level 3 Data

PCE
Level 3 data applies statistical outlier checks in order to screen values for statistical anomalies. Mean Absolute Deviation (MAD) was calculated for the following columns, then strong and extreme outliers were flagged/removed from the data.
Outlier Detection (MAD)

Method: Median Absolute Deviation (MAD)
Threshold Multiplier: 3 MADs from the median




Columns checked for outliers




residential_rate


pce_eligible_residential_kwh


pce_eligible_community_kwh


most_recent_fuel_purch_price


fuel_used_gallons


diesel_kwh_generated


hydro_kwh_generated


natural_gas_kwh_generated


wind_kwh_generated


solar_kwh_generated


total_kwh_purchased


powerhouse_consumption_kwh


peak_consumption_kw


residential_sold_to


commercial_sold_to


com_facil_sold_to


govt_facil_sold_to


unbilled_sold_to


residential_customers


commercial_customers


com_facil_customers


govt_facil_customers


unbilled_customers

Level 2 Data
Level 2 data processing applies basic transformations to create or modify columns.

PCE








File Name
Size
Download




l2_pce.csv
6.06M
Download CSV



 



Level 1 Data
Level 1 data is created by running basic data quality checks against the data and overwriting with NULL for values outside the boundaries. For instance, the column most_recent_fuel_purch_price requires values to be greater than $0.50 and less than $10.00. If a record contains a value of $0.20 or $50.00, the value is discarded. A list of records with out-of-bound values can be seen on Quality and Outliers

PCE
Data Quality Bounds



Column
Allowed Range / Bounds




non_fuel_expenses
≥ 0 (No Zeros)


powerhouse_consumption_kwh
≥ 0 (No Zeros)


peak_consumption_kw
≥ 0 (No Zeros)


most_recent_fuel_purch_price
0.5 to 10


diesel_kwh_generated
≥ 0 (No Zeros)


residential_sold_to
≥ 0 (No Zeros)


commercial_sold_to
≥ 0


com_facil_sold_to
≥ 0


govt_facil_sold_to
≥ 0


unbilled_sold_to
≥ 0

Monthly Power Cost Equalization (PCE)
Below are links to download archived copies of raw monthly data exported by the Alaska Energy Authority in XLSX format. These files are unprocessed and may contain errors. Use with caution.
